title: Navigating Windows
subtitle: {key:Ctrl-W} {key:h}/{key:j}/{key:k}/{key:l} — move between splits. {key:Ctrl-W} {key:c} — close. {key:Ctrl-W} {key:o} — only.
summary: Ctrl-W with a direction key moves to the next window in that direction. Ctrl-W c closes the current window. Ctrl-W o closes everything except the current window.

blocks.text: {key:Ctrl-W} is the *window* prefix. After it, {key:h}/{key:j}/{key:k}/{key:l} jump to the window in that direction. {key:c} closes; {key:o} keeps only this one.

blocks.title: Closing a Window vs Closing a Buffer
blocks.text:
{key:Ctrl-W}{key:c} (or {key::}{key:c}{key:l}{key:o}{key:s}{key:e}) closes the **window** — the viewport. The buffer it was showing stays loaded; you can re-show it in another window with {key::}{key:b} {bufname}.

{key::}{key:b}{key:d} (`:bdelete`) closes the **buffer** — unloads the file from memory. The buffer disappears from {key::}{key:l}{key:s}.

{key::}{key:q} is a hybrid that confuses newcomers: it closes the current window, *and* if that was the last window showing the buffer, may also unload it depending on settings. Use {key::}{key:c}{key:l}{key:o}{key:s}{key:e} when you mean window-only and {key::}{key:b}{key:d} when you mean buffer-only.

{key:Ctrl-W}{key:o}: "only-this-window." Closes every other window, leaving the current one full-screen. Buffers are not unloaded.
Most users remap window navigation to plain {key:Ctrl-h}/{key:Ctrl-j}/{key:Ctrl-k}/{key:Ctrl-l} (no prefix). It's the single highest-impact remap for a multi-split workflow.
